pages.title: Disclaimer
pages.text:
Disclaimer

DISCLAIMER: Unless you have already signed a Retainer Agreement with our firm, filling out this questionnaire does not create an attorney-client relationship. The information provided in this questionnaire will allow us to review your case and give you a quote. Should you wish to retain us after we review your information and contact you, any representation is subject to a signed Retainer Agreement.

pages.title: Brodsky Law PLLC
pages.text:
Brodsky Law PLLC
Divorce questionnaire
Welcome! These questions will help us collect the information needed for your divorce case.

pages.title: ABOUT YOU AND YOUR SPOUSE
pages.text:
ABOUT YOU AND YOUR SPOUSE
The following questions are about you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.

pages.title: About this form
pages.text:
About this form
This form has two (2) steps.
1. Part A: Starting the Divorce Case and
2. Part B: Getting Your Divorce Case on the Court Calendar.
You must complete the paper work from Part A first and then come back to complete the paper work for Part B.

pages.title: Do you have an index number for your divorce case?
pages.text:
Do you have an index number for your divorce case?
This means an index number was obtained from the court (about $210), a fee waiver was approved, or court fees were not required, and a Summons With Notice or a Summons and Verified Complaint to start your case.

pages.title: Did you use this form before to make your divorce papers?
pages.text: Did you use this form before to make your divorce papers?

pages.title: Did you save your answers the last time you used this form?
pages.text: Did you save your answers the last time you used this form?

pages.title: You can use your answers from your last visit for this part. "Exit" the form and Log In.
pages.text:
You can use your answers from your last visit for this part. "Exit" the form and Log In.
Click "Continue" if you don't remember your log in information, if you didn't save your answers, or you are having trouble with getting to your saved answers.

pages.title: Residency Requirement
pages.text:
Residency Requirement
To file for a divorce in New York, you must meet a residency requirement.
Have you lived in New York State continuously for at least 2 years?

pages.title: Your Information
pages.text:
Your Information
What is your address?

pages.title: When you finish this form, you will be able to print Instructions and the papers you need for your uncontested divorce.
pages.text:
When you finish this form, you will be able to print Instructions and the papers you need for your uncontested divorce. Print on only one side of the paper.
Court filing is handled by Brodsky Law; you do not file these papers yourself.

pages.title: In some browsers the backspace button will not work to delete or change an answer.
pages.text:
In some browsers the backspace button will not work to delete or change an answer.
If this happens, use your mouse to highlight the information you need to change and then use the delete key to remove the letters before changing the information.

pages.title: Has %%[Defendant current name TE]%% lived in New York State continuously for at least two years?
pages.text: Has %%[Defendant current name TE]%% lived in New York State continuously for at least two years?

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: Your Information
pages.text:
Your Information
What is your address in prison?

pages.title: How to Use this form
pages.text:
How to Use this form
You can click on any word that is in BLUE for a definition. You can also click on "Learn More" for more information.
You can click "Back" in the top left corner to go back and change your answers.

pages.title: This form will help you complete the uncontested divorce forms.
pages.text: This form will help you complete the uncontested divorce forms.

pages.title: IMPORTANT
pages.text:
IMPORTANT
It is very important that you enter all of your information correctly. Be very careful that all names, addresses and dates are right.
Correcting this information after your case is filed is a complicated process and will delay your divorce.

pages.title: What is your BOP number in %%[Plaintiff prison name TE]%%?
pages.text:
What is your BOP number in %%[Plaintiff prison name TE]%%?
The Bureau of Prison Register Number format is 99999-999

pages.title: IMPORTANT
pages.text:
IMPORTANT
Tell the truth when you answer these questions.
Most of the documents for a divorce are sworn statements. That means you swear the information in it is the truth. It is crime to lie or make a "sworn false statement." This crime is called perjury.

pages.title: What is your DIN number in %%[Plaintiff prison name TE]%%?
pages.text:
What is your DIN number in %%[Plaintiff prison name TE]%%?
The DIN has a format of
99-A-9999.

pages.title: Is this form For Me?
pages.text:
Is this form For Me?
You can use this form if:
(1) there are no children under 21 years of age that were born or adopted by you and the Defendant before or during the marriage AND
(2) your marriage has "broken down irretrievably" for at least six months and the marriage can't be saved, also known as no fault, AND
(3) all issues have been settled

pages.title: What is your identification number in %%[Plaintiff prison name TE]%%?
pages.text:
What is your identification number in %%[Plaintiff prison name TE]%%?
This number can be called the book & case number, register number, etc

pages.title: Is this the address where you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% lived together while you were married?
pages.text: Is this the address where you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% lived together while you were married?

pages.title: Do you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% own this home together or are both of your names on the rental lease?
pages.text: Do you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% own this home together or are both of your names on the rental lease?

pages.title: You can ask the Judge to give you "exclusive occupancy of the marital residence". The Judge will make the decision as pa
pages.text:
You can ask the Judge to give you "exclusive occupancy of the marital residence". The Judge will make the decision as part of the divorce Judgment.
This means that you want to stay in your home and you want %%[Defendant current name TE]%% to leave.
Do you want to ask for "exclusive occupancy of the marital residence"?

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: WARNING:
pages.text:
WARNING:
DO NOT USE this form if you want to keep your address or social security number private.
This means that you want to hide your address or social security number from the person you are divorcing.
Your address and social security number are printed on these papers. Speak to a Supreme Court Clerk's Office for help on how to keep this information private.

pages.title: Have you lived in New York State continuously for at least one year?
pages.text: Have you lived in New York State continuously for at least one year?

pages.text: What is your phone number and email address?

pages.title: Has %%[Defendant current name TE]%% lived in New York State continuously for at least one year?
pages.text: Has %%[Defendant current name TE]%% lived in New York State continuously for at least one year?

pages.title: Order of Protection
pages.text:
Order of Protection
If you need an Order of Protection from the Defendant, Exit this form and go to the Family Court in your county. You can get a temporary Order of Protection to protect yourself immediately.

pages.title: If your marriage is a common law marriage, you can't use this form.
pages.text:
If your marriage is a common law marriage, you can't use this form.
Exit this form and contact the Supreme Court Clerk's Office for more information.

pages.title: Are you incarcerated?
pages.text:
Are you incarcerated?
This means that you are in prison right now.

pages.title: Did you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% get married in New York State?
pages.text: Did you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% get married in New York State?

pages.title: Do you have a social security number?
pages.text: Do you have a social security number?

pages.title: Are both you and the Defendant over 18 years of age?
pages.text: Are both you and the Defendant over 18 years of age?

pages.title: This form is not for you.
pages.text:
This form is not for you.
You and the person you want to divorce must be at least 18 years old to use this form.

pages.title: How many children do you and the Defendant have together?
pages.text:
How many children do you and the Defendant have together?
Count all children who were born or adopted by both of you before or during the marriage.

pages.title: %%[Is your child/ren TE]%% 20 years old or younger?
pages.text: %%[Is your child/ren TE]%% 20 years old or younger?

pages.title: %%[Is your child/ren TE]%% emancipated?
pages.text:
%%[Is your child/ren TE]%% emancipated?
This means that %%[all child/ren TE]%% in the military or married, or 17 years old or younger and self-supporting.

pages.title: This form is not for you.
pages.text:
This form is not for you.
this form can only be used if you and the person you want to divorce don't have children.

pages.title: In a divorce case, the court is only interested in "children of the marriage."
pages.text:
In a divorce case, the court is only interested in "children of the marriage."
Your %%[child/ren TE]%% %%[child/ren is/are TE]%% 21 years old or older or emancipated. For a divorce case, this means that you have no “children of the marriage” and the papers will say that "There are no children of the marriage."

pages.title: Did you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% live in New York State while you were married?
pages.text: Did you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% live in New York State while you were married?

pages.title: What is your social security number?
pages.text: What is your social security number?

pages.title: Do both you and the Defendant live in the United States?
pages.text: Do both you and the Defendant live in the United States?

pages.title: This form is not for you.
pages.text:
This form is not for you.
To use this form, both you and the person you want to divorce must live in the United States.

pages.title: Do you or the Defendant already have a divorce case waiting for a decision in this court or any other court?
pages.text: Do you or the Defendant already have a divorce case waiting for a decision in this court or any other court?

pages.title: This form is not for you.
pages.text:
This form is not for you.
If you and the person you want to divorce have a divorce case pending in this court or in any other court, you can't use this form.

pages.title: Do you know where the Defendant lives or works right now?
pages.text: Do you know where the Defendant lives or works right now?

pages.title: This form is not for you.
pages.text:
This form is not for you.
The court papers must be personally delivered to the person you want to divorce. The Defendant's address is required to use this form.

pages.title: Settlement Agreement
pages.text:
Settlement Agreement
Did you and the Defendant sign a written Settlement Agreement?
Sometimes the Settlement Agreement is called a Stipulation of Settlement or a Property Settlement and Separation Agreement.

pages.title: The written Settlement Agreement is filed with the court when the index number is issued. There is an additional fee of
pages.text: The written Settlement Agreement is filed with the court when the index number is issued. There is an additional fee of about $35.

pages.title: Marital Property
pages.text:
Marital Property
To use the grounds of no fault, the division of marital property must be agreed to between you and the Defendant.
This means that you and the Defendant must agree between yourselves how the property and debt is divided.

pages.title: This form is not for you at this time.
pages.text:
This form is not for you at this time.
For a no fault divorce, you must both agree on all marital property issues.

pages.title: Marital property is anything that was earned or acquired during your marriage. It does not matter if you own it together
pages.text:
Marital property is anything that was earned or acquired during your marriage. It does not matter if you own it together or alone.
Examples of marital property: pension, annuity, real estate like a house, condo or co-op, retirement account, 401(k), 403(b), IRA, bank accounts, savings account, debt (credit cards, store cards, car payments, etc.), car, boat, etc.

pages.title: Do you and the Defendant have any marital property?
pages.text: Do you and the Defendant have any marital property?

pages.title: Have you and the Defendant talked about the marital property and already divided the property?
pages.text: Have you and the Defendant talked about the marital property and already divided the property?

pages.title: Are you going to contest any marital property?
pages.text:
Are you going to contest any marital property?
This means that you will not agree with the Defendant on how the property or debt will be divided between you.

pages.title: Is the Defendant going to contest any marital property?
pages.text:
Is the Defendant going to contest any marital property?
This means that the Defendant will not agree on how the property or debt will be divided between you.

pages.title: This form is not for you at this time.
pages.text:
This form is not for you at this time.
Before you can use this form, you and the Defendant must agree on what will happen to the marital property after you get your divorce.

pages.title: You can use alternate dispute resolution (ADR) or any form of mediation (assistance) to help you and the Defendant divid
pages.text:
You can use alternate dispute resolution (ADR) or any form of mediation (assistance) to help you and the Defendant divide your property.
ADR is a way of ending conflict without going to court. There are different types of ADR such as Collaborative Family Law and divorce mediation.

pages.title: Collaborative Family Law is a type of ADR where each person in a divorce has a specially trained lawyer to end a marriag
pages.text:
Collaborative Family Law is a type of ADR where each person in a divorce has a specially trained lawyer to end a marriage respectfully and without going to court.
In divorce mediation, a person called a "mediator" helps a couple work out the issues of their divorce to reach a settlement they both agree on. Mediation is not for everyone.
There is a fee for ADR services but sometimes, depending on your income, there is a reduced fee.

pages.title: Retirement Plans
pages.text:
Retirement Plans
During the marriage, did you or the Defendant put money into a retirement account?
This means that you and/or the Defendant contributed to a "defined contribution plan," such as a public or private pension plan, IRA, 401(k) plans, 403(b) plans, 457 plans, Thrift Savings Plans, employee stock ownership plans, or profit-sharing plans.

pages.title: If the Defendant does not sign the Affirmation of Defendant and return it to you then he or she will "default".
pages.text:
If the Defendant does not sign the Affirmation of Defendant and return it to you then he or she will "default".
If the Defendant defaults, the Judge may not sign the divorce Judgment because the retirement account issue has not been agreed on by both of you.

pages.title: You are welcome to continue. However, if the Defendant does not sign the Affirmation of Defendant and return it to you t
pages.text:
You are welcome to continue. However, if the Defendant does not sign the Affirmation of Defendant and return it to you then he or she will "default".
If the Defendant defaults, the Judge may not sign the divorce Judgment because the retirement account issue, if there is one, has not been agreed on by both of you.

pages.title: Under New York law, the portion of some retirement accounts earned during the marriage are considered marital property.
pages.text:
Under New York law, the portion of some retirement accounts earned during the marriage are considered marital property.
This means that the retirement accounts can be divided no matter whose name is on it when the marriage ends.

pages.title: Do you have a Domestic Relations Order or Qualified Domestic Relations Order ("DRO" or "QDRO")?
pages.text: Do you have a Domestic Relations Order or Qualified Domestic Relations Order ("DRO" or "QDRO")?

pages.title: Do you and the Defendant have any kind of written agreement (like a written stipulation or written Settlement Agreement)
pages.text: Do you and the Defendant have any kind of written agreement (like a written stipulation or written Settlement Agreement) that says if the retirement accounts will be divided and how?

pages.title: If you have a written agreement or stipulation, file it when you submit the divorce papers.
pages.text:
If you have a written agreement or stipulation, file it when you submit the divorce papers.
If you have a DRO or QDRO, it can be filed when you file the divorce papers. The Judge will sign it at the time the divorce judgment is signed, if you are given a divorce. After the Judge signs the DRO or QDRO, it must be filed with your plan administrator.

pages.title: Who has a retirement account?
pages.text: Who has a retirement account?

pages.title: Do you "waive" your rights to the Defendant's retirement accounts (if any)?
pages.text:
Do you "waive" your rights to the Defendant's retirement accounts (if any)?
This means that you will not ask for any part of the Defendant's retirement account in the future.

pages.title: Will the Defendant "waive" his or her rights to your retirement accounts (if any)?
pages.text:
Will the Defendant "waive" his or her rights to your retirement accounts (if any)?
This means that the Defendant will not ask for any part of your retirement account in the future.

pages.title: The issue of what will happen to the retirement accounts must be agreed on by you and the person you are divorcing befor
pages.text:
The issue of what will happen to the retirement accounts must be agreed on by you and the person you are divorcing before you use this form.
If you or the Defendant will not come to a written agreement about your retirement accounts or "waive" your rights to each other's accounts, the case will no longer be an uncontested matter. This means that you can't use this form.

pages.title: Will you be asking for maintenance?
pages.text: Will you be asking for maintenance?

pages.title: This form is not for you.
pages.text:
This form is not for you.
this form can only be used if you are asking for maintenance and there is a Settlement Agreement between you and your spouse.

pages.title: Do you get public assistance?
pages.text: Do you get public assistance?

pages.title: Did the breakdown of the marriage happen in New York State?
pages.text: Did the breakdown of the marriage happen in New York State?

pages.title: About Getting a Divorce
pages.text:
About Getting a Divorce
There are 2 parts to getting a divorce and this form will help you with the papers and procedures in each part.

pages.title: Do both you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% live in New York State right now?
pages.text: Do both you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% live in New York State right now?

pages.title: Part A: Starting the Divorce Case
pages.text:
Part A: Starting the Divorce Case
To start a case, an index number is obtained from the court ($210) and counsel files the Summons With Notice or the Summons and Verified Complaint with the court.
The person you want to divorce is called the Defendant.

pages.title: This form is not for you.
pages.text:
This form is not for you.
You do not meet any of the residency requirements needed to file for a divorce in New York State.

pages.title: If the Defendant signs and returns the Affirmation of Defendant to you, you can continue on to Part B and get your divor
pages.text:
If the Defendant signs and returns the Affirmation of Defendant to you, you can continue on to Part B and get your divorce case on the court calendar.
If the Defendant does not sign and return the Affirmation of Defendant to you, the paperwork must be personally delivered to ("served on") the Defendant by someone, but not you.

pages.title: From the date the Defendant was served, s/he has 20 days or 30 days to answer. If the Defendant answers and does not agr
pages.text: From the date the Defendant was served, s/he has 20 days or 30 days to answer. If the Defendant answers and does not agree to the divorce, it is no longer an uncontested matter. If the Defendant does not answer during this time, s/he has defaulted and you can go on to Part B.

pages.title: Part B: Getting Your Divorce Case on the Court Calendar
pages.text:
Part B: Getting Your Divorce Case on the Court Calendar
After the Defendant returns the Affirmation of Defendant to you or after 40 days from the date of service, your attorney files a second set of forms with the court.
These papers are stamped by a clerk and your case is assigned to a Judge or a Special Referee. There is a $125 court fee at this time.

pages.title: It may take some time for the Judge to look at your case.
pages.text:
It may take some time for the Judge to look at your case.
If the Judge finds that there is something wrong or incomplete with the papers, the court will let you know so that you can come in and correct them.

pages.title: If the Judge signs the Judgment of Divorce, the court will contact you. The Judgment must be filed with the court.
pages.text: If the Judge signs the Judgment of Divorce, the court will contact you. The Judgment must be filed with the court.

pages.title: It costs at least $335 to file for divorce.
pages.text:
It costs at least $335 to file for divorce.
If you do not have the money to pay the court fees, you may be able to request a fee waiver from the court.

pages.title: Your Spouse's Information
pages.text:
Your Spouse's Information

What is your spouse's address?

*If you do not know your spouse's current address, please provide their last known address*

pages.title: Your Grounds for the Divorce
pages.text:
Your Grounds for the Divorce
To file for divorce in New York State, you must have a legal reason for the divorce. This is called the "grounds" for your divorce.
New York is a 'no-fault' divorce state, meaning that neither spouse necessarily be 'at fault' for the divorce. Rather, the grounds for divorce is the marriage being 'broken down irretrievably' for at least 6 months.

pages.title: Irretrievable Breakdown of Marriage
pages.text:
Irretrievable Breakdown of Marriage
In the papers, you must swear that your relationship has "broken down irretrievably" for at least six months.
This means that it is impossible for you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% to save or fix the marriage and stay together.

pages.title: Have the following with you while you are answering the questions:
pages.text:
Have the following with you while you are answering the questions:
1. Marriage certificate
2. Date when you were married
3. Place where you were married
4. Your social security number
5. The Defendant's address
6. The Defendant's social security number

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s Information
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s Information
What is %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s address in prison?

pages.title: Irretrievable Breakdown of Marriage
pages.text:
Irretrievable Breakdown of Marriage

Has your relationship with %%[Defendant current name TE]%% "broken down irretrievably" for at least 6 months?

This means that your marriage to %%[Defendant current name TE]%% has been over for at least 6 months and your relationship can't be saved.

pages.title: It is useful to have a Copy of the Summons with Notice or the Summons and Complaint with you while you are answering the
pages.text: It is useful to have a Copy of the Summons with Notice or the Summons and Complaint with you while you are answering the questions.

pages.title: This form is not for you at this time.
pages.text:
This form is not for you at this time.
Your marriage with %%[Defendant current name TE]%% must have "broken down irretrievably" for at least 6 months before your case can proceed using this questionnaire. That means that your relationship can't be fixed.
You can exit this form or continue. If you continue, the case cannot be filed until it has been 6 months since your relationship ended.

pages.text:
You can ask the Judge to give you "exclusive occupancy of the marital residence". The Judge will make the decision as part of the divorce Judgment.
This means that you want to return to your home and you want %%[Defendant current name TE]%% to leave.
Do you want to ask for "exclusive occupancy of the marital residence"?

If no, there may be extra fees for divorce by publication.

pages.title: Settlement Agreement
pages.text:
Settlement Agreement
You and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% signed a written Settlement Agreement.

pages.title: IMPORTANT
pages.text:
IMPORTANT
Your name and the name of the person you want to divorce must be typed in exactly as it appears on your marriage certificate.
If the names on the paperwork are not correct, the court may not enter your judgment and your divorce will be delayed.

pages.title: IMPORTANT
pages.text:
IMPORTANT
Your name and the name of the person you want to divorce must be typed in exactly as it appears in the papers already on file with the court.
If the names are not typed exactly as it appears in the papers already on file with the court, the court will not enter your judgment and your divorce will be delayed.

pages.title: It would be helpful if you have it with you to answer the following questions.
pages.text: It would be helpful if you have it with you to answer the following questions.

pages.title: What is %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s social security number?
pages.text: What is %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s social security number?

pages.title: Type in your name exactly as it appears on the marriage certificate.
pages.text: Type in your name exactly as it appears on the marriage certificate.

pages.title: What is the date of the written Settlement Agreement?
pages.text: What is the date of the written Settlement Agreement?

pages.title: Why can't you supply your spouse's social security number?
pages.text: Why can't you supply your spouse's social security number?

pages.title: Did you get a legal name change while you were married?
pages.text:
Did you get a legal name change while you were married?
This means that during the marriage you changed your name from the name on the marriage certificate.

pages.title: You got a legal name change during the marriage.
pages.text:
You got a legal name change during the marriage.
What is your current legal name?

pages.title: Include proof of any name change when the papers are filed.
pages.text:
Include proof of any name change when the papers are filed.
A proof of name change is a legal document that verifies that a person has legally changed their name, such as a court order granting the name change or a certificate of naturalization or citizenship.

pages.title: Who helped you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% write the Settlement Agreement?
pages.text: Who helped you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% write the Settlement Agreement?

pages.title: It is important that the name on the photo ID is the same as the name printed on the divorce papers or the notary may no
pages.text:
It is important that the name on the photo ID is the same as the name printed on the divorce papers or the notary may not notarize the papers.
The notary will check your identification to ensure that you are who you say you are. You may need to show a government-issued ID, such as a driver's license or passport.

pages.title: Health Insurance
pages.text:
Health Insurance
According to the written Settlement Agreement, is there a statement about future health care coverage for you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: Are you in prison?
pages.text: Are you in prison?

pages.title: There is a legal requirement that the written Settlement Agreement states an arrangement for future health care coverage
pages.text:
There is a legal requirement that the written Settlement Agreement states an arrangement for future health care coverage for you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.
An addendum to the written Settlement Agreement which satisfies this legal requirement should be signed by you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%%, notarized and filed with your papers. This form will be printed with your other papers.

pages.title: You are in prison.
pages.text:
You are in prison.
To use this form, you must know your identification number used in prison. This number is called the DIN, BOP, Book & Case number or register number depending on where you are incarcerated.

pages.title: Maintenance
pages.text:
Maintenance
What does the written Settlement Agreement say about maintenance?

pages.title: According to the written Settlement Agreement, how much maintenance is to be paid?
pages.text: According to the written Settlement Agreement, how much maintenance is to be paid?

pages.title: How often is the maintenance to be paid?
pages.text:
How often is the maintenance to be paid?
For example: every 6 months, once a year on December 1st.

pages.text: Do you know %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s phone number?

pages.text: Type in the name of the person you want to divorce as it appears on the marriage certificate.

pages.text: What is %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s phone number and email address?

pages.title: Did %%[Defendant current name TE]%% get a legal name change while you were married?
pages.text:
Did %%[Defendant current name TE]%% get a legal name change while you were married?
This means that during the marriage %%[Defendant current name TE]%% changed their name from the name on the marriage certificate.

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% got a legal name change during the marriage.
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%% got a legal name change during the marriage.
What is %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s current legal name?

pages.title: You will have to show the court proof that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% got a legal name change during the marriage.
pages.text:
You will have to show the court proof that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% got a legal name change during the marriage.
If you can, include a copy of the signed Name Change Order or their Naturalization Certificate with your papers.

pages.title: Is %%[Defendant current name TE]%% in the military and on active duty?
pages.text: Is %%[Defendant current name TE]%% in the military and on active duty?

pages.title: Military law protects service members. Because %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is on active duty, the judge can postpone
pages.text:
Military law protects service members. Because %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is on active duty, the judge can postpone the case until %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% returns.
However, %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can let the divorce case go forward without delay if %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% signs the Affirmation of Defendant and returns it to you.
Do you think %%[Defendant current name TE]%% will sign the Affirmation of Defendant?

pages.title: It is important that papers are delivered in a certain time period after filing or you will have to start over and buy a
pages.text:
It is important that papers are delivered in a certain time period after filing or you will have to start over and buy another index number.
If you are not sure if %%[Defendant current name TE]%% will sign the Affirmation of Defendant, speak to the Supreme Court Clerk's Office for more information.

pages.title: Is %%[Defendant current name TE]%% in prison?
pages.text:
Is %%[Defendant current name TE]%% in prison?
This means that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is in a federal, state or local correctional institution.

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is in prison.
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%% is in prison.
To use this form, you must know %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s identification number used in prison. This number is called the DIN, BOP, Book & Case number or register number depending on where %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% is incarcerated.

pages.title: The court papers must be "served on" %%[Defendant current name TE]%%. This means that the papers must be personally deli
pages.text:
The court papers must be "served on" %%[Defendant current name TE]%%. This means that the papers must be personally delivered to %%[Defendant current name TE]%% in prison.
Contact the prison where %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% is incarcerated for more information about how to do this.

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: As far as you know, does %%[Defendant current name TE]%% get public assistance?
pages.text: As far as you know, does %%[Defendant current name TE]%% get public assistance?

pages.title: Marital Property
pages.text:
Marital Property
Pick one:

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is serving in the military.
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%% is serving in the military.
Which military service branch is %%[Defendant current name TE]%% serving with?

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is not serving in the military. How do you know this?
pages.text: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is not serving in the military. How do you know this?

pages.text: On what date did you ask %%[Defendant current name TE]%% whether or not %%HESHE([Defendant Gender])%% is serving in the military?

pages.title: About Your Marriage
pages.text:
About Your Marriage
When were you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% married?

pages.title: 12a.1-Ancillary relief
Ancillary Relief
pages.text:
Ancillary Relief
You may have asked the Judge to decide other things as part of your divorce case when you filed your %%[Initiating papers TE]%%.
It would be helpful if you had the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% with you to answer the following questions.

pages.title: Your Last Name
pages.text:
Your Last Name
When you got married, you may have changed your last name.
Did your last name change when you got married?

pages.title: List your other last name before you got married:
pages.text:
List your other last name before you got married:

(If you have more than one, enter one now. We will add the rest later.)

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s Last Name
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s Last Name
When %%[Defendant current name TE]%% got married, %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% may have changed %%HISHER([Defendant gender])%% last name.
Did %%[Defendant current name TE]%% change %%HISHER([Defendant gender])%% last name when %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% married you?

pages.title: List %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s other last name before marriage:
pages.text: List %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s other last name before marriage:

pages.title: Spousal Support
pages.text:
Spousal Support
Is there a spousal support court order between you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% at this time?
In New York, this court order would be from Family Court.

pages.title: Were you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% married in the United States?
pages.text: Were you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% married in the United States?

pages.title: Spousal support is paid and received only while you are married. The support order ends when you get divorced.
pages.text:
Spousal support is paid and received only while you are married. The support order ends when you get divorced.
Support payments after you are divorced is called maintenance. The Judge decides if maintenance will be paid and for how long.
Are you asking the Judge for spousal support to continue as maintenance after the divorce?

pages.title: Where were you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% married?
pages.text: Where were you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% married?

pages.title: Where were you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% married?
pages.text: Where were you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% married?

pages.title: Which court is the spousal support order from?
pages.text: Which court is the spousal support order from?

pages.title: The spousal support is from another court. I need some information about the court order.
pages.text: The spousal support is from another court. I need some information about the court order.

pages.title: I need more information about the spousal support order.
pages.text:
I need more information about the spousal support order.
Where is the %%[Spousal support court MC]%% Court located and what was the docket number?

pages.title: According to the spousal support order, who pays the spousal support?
pages.text: According to the spousal support order, who pays the spousal support?

pages.title: According to the spousal support order, how much support is paid and how often?
pages.text: According to the spousal support order, how much support is paid and how often?

pages.title: According to the spousal support order, how often is the support to be paid?
pages.text:
According to the spousal support order, how often is the support to be paid?
For example: once a year on December 1st, every six months, etc.

pages.title: The Judge decides if maintenance is paid and for how long.
pages.text:
The Judge decides if maintenance is paid and for how long.
After you are divorced, how much maintenance are you asking for?

pages.title: How often would you like the maintenance to be paid?
pages.text:
How often would you like the maintenance to be paid?
For example: once a year on December 1st, every six months, etc.

pages.title: Maintenance is usually for a set period of time. How long should the maintenance be paid?
pages.text: Maintenance is usually for a set period of time. How long should the maintenance be paid?

pages.title: How long would you like the maintenance be paid?
pages.text:
How long would you like the maintenance be paid?
From the date of divorce, maintenance should be paid

pages.title: Since you are asking for maintenance to be paid, the Judge will want to know about your income and %%[Defendant current
pages.text:
Since you are asking for maintenance to be paid, the Judge will want to know about your income and %%[Defendant current name TE]%%'s income.
What is your income every month?

pages.title: Where does your monthly income come from?
pages.text:
Where does your monthly income come from?
For example: from work, unemployment, disability, etc.

pages.title: Were you married in a religious ceremony?
pages.text: Were you married in a religious ceremony?

pages.title: Order of Protection
pages.text:
Order of Protection
Do you have an Order of Protection against %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
This means that there is an Order of Protection where you are named as the Petitioner and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is named as the Respondent.

pages.title: Tell me about the Order of Protection. Give the following information: county and court it is from, date of the order an
pages.text: Tell me about the Order of Protection. Give the following information: county and court it is from, date of the order and the index or docket number.

pages.title: The court requires that you do everything needed by the religion so that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can remarry aft
pages.text:
The court requires that you do everything needed by the religion so that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can remarry after the divorce.
In some religions, you may not have to do anything but the court still requires that you swear before the court's final judgment.

pages.title: Does %%[Defendant current name TE]%% have an Order of Protection against you?
pages.text:
Does %%[Defendant current name TE]%% have an Order of Protection against you?
This means that there is an Order of Protection where %%[Defendant current name TE]%% is named as the Petitioner and you are named as the Respondent.

pages.title: Tell me about the Order of Protection. Give the following information: county and court it is from, date of the order an
pages.text: Tell me about the Order of Protection. Give the following information: county and court it is from, date of the order and the index or docket number.

pages.title: Preparing your next steps...
pages.text: Preparing your next steps...

pages.title: Other Court Orders
pages.text:
Other Court Orders
Are there any other court orders (that is not a spousal support order or an order of protection) between you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: How many other court orders are there between you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
pages.text: How many other court orders are there between you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: Tell me about the first court order.
pages.text:
Tell me about the first court order.
Give the following information: What the court order is for, name of court, county where the court is located, the date the order was signed and the index or docket number of the file.

pages.title: Tell me about the next court order.
pages.text:
Tell me about the next court order.
Give the following information: What the court order is for, name of court, county where the court is located, the date the order was signed and the index or docket number of the file.

pages.title: Tell me about the next court order.
pages.text:
Tell me about the next court order.
Give the following information: What the court order is for, name of court, county where the court is located, the date the order was signed and the index or docket number of the file.

pages.title: 16a-Other relief
What else would you like to ask for?
pages.text: What else would you like to ask for?

pages.title: Other Relief
pages.text:
Other Relief
Look at the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%. There is a section about Ancillary Relief.
Did you ask the court for anything else?

pages.title: What else did you ask for?
pages.text: What else did you ask for?

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: Filing County
pages.text:
Filing County

In what county are you filing for divorce?

pages.title: Who lives in %%[Jurisdiction county MC]%% County?
pages.text: Who lives in %%[Jurisdiction county MC]%% County?

pages.title: Initiating Papers
pages.text:
Initiating Papers
You have the choice to start your divorce case with Summons and Verified Complaint or with a Summons With Notice.
One difference between starting your case with a Summons and Verified Complaint and Summons With Notice is the amount of details you want to bring to the court at the beginning of your case.
Most counties require that you file a Verified Complaint at some point.

pages.title: How do you want to start your uncontested divorce case?
pages.text:
How do you want to start your uncontested divorce case?
Pick one:

pages.title: Finished
pages.text:
Finished
You have finished Part A: Starting the Divorce Case.

pages.id: A01a.2-Edit A or go to B
pages.title: What would you like to do?
pages.text: What would you like to do?

pages.title: What papers did you file with to start your case?
pages.text: What papers did you file with to start your case?

pages.title: You must meet the residency requirement to file for a divorce in New York State.
pages.text:
You must meet the residency requirement to file for a divorce in New York State.
Look at your Verified Complaint in the paragraph that starts with "Second".

pages.title: Do you have an index number for your divorce case?
pages.text:
Do you have an index number for your divorce case?
This means an index number was obtained from the court (about $210), a fee waiver was approved, or court fees were not required, and a %%[Initiating papers TE]%% to start your case.

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The Plaintiff has resided in New York State for a continuous period of at least two years immediately preceding the commencement of this divorce action."

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The Defendant has resided in New York State for a continuous period of at least two years immediately preceding the commencement of this divorce action."

pages.id: A03b.1-Part A but no index number
pages.title: To start a divorce case, an index number is required and the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% is filed with the court.
pages.text:
To start a divorce case, an index number is required and the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% is filed with the court.
Would you like to print the Part A: Starting Your Divorce papers again?

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The Plaintiff resided in New York State on the date of commencement of this divorce action and for a continuous period of one year immediately preceding the commencement of this divorce action and the parties were married in New York State."

pages.title: To start a divorce case, an index number is required and the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% is filed with the court.
pages.text:
To start a divorce case, an index number is required and the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% is filed with the court.
Would you like to make the Part A: Starting Your Divorce papers again?

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The Defendant resided in New York State on the date of commencement of this divorce action and for a continuous period of one year immediately preceding the commencement of this divorce action and the parties were married in New York State."

pages.title: On what date were the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% filed with the court?
pages.text:
On what date were the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% filed with the court?
The court clerk "time-stamped" the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% with the time and date when you filed the papers.

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The Plaintiff resided in New York State on the date of commencement of this divorce action and for a continuous period of one year immediately preceding the commencement of this divorce action and the parties have resided as married people in New York State."

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The Defendant resided in New York State on the date of commencement of this divorce action and for a continuous period of one year immediately preceding the commencement of this divorce action and the parties have resided as married people in New York State."

pages.title: Were the papers filed on the same day you received your index number?
pages.text: Were the papers filed on the same day you received your index number?

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The cause of action occurred in New York State and both parties were residents at the time of commencement of this divorce action."

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The cause of action occurred in New York State and Plaintiff resided in New York State for a continuous period of at least one year immediately preceding the commencement of this divorce action."

pages.title: Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
pages.text:
Is the following in the SECOND paragraph of the Verified Complaint?
"The cause of action occurred in New York State and Defendant resided in New York State for a continuous period of at least one year immediately preceding the commencement of this divorce action."

pages.title: You must meet the residency requirement to file for a divorce in New York State.
pages.text:
You must meet the residency requirement to file for a divorce in New York State.
Look at your Verified Complaint again and the paragraph that starts with "SECOND".

pages.title: On what day did you get your index number?
pages.text: On what day did you get your index number?

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: Was the Affirmation of Defendant form and instructions given to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
pages.text: Was the Affirmation of Defendant form and instructions given to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: Did %%[Defendant current name TE]%% return the Affirmation of Defendant signed to you?
pages.text: Did %%[Defendant current name TE]%% return the Affirmation of Defendant signed to you?

pages.title: The divorce procedure is easier if %%[Defendant current name TE]%% returns the Affirmation of Defendant signed to you.
pages.text:
The divorce procedure is easier if %%[Defendant current name TE]%% returns the Affirmation of Defendant signed to you.
If %%[Defendant current name TE]%% does not return the Affirmation of Defendant to you, then the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%, the Notice of Automatic Orders, and the Notice Concerning Continuation of Health Care Coverage must be served on %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.

pages.title: Was the Affirmation of Defendant given to %%[Defendant current name TE]%% somewhere in New York State?
pages.text: Was the Affirmation of Defendant given to %%[Defendant current name TE]%% somewhere in New York State?

pages.title: On what date were the papers given to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
pages.text:
On what date were the papers given to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
The date is found on the Affirmation of Defendant, in the second paragraph.

pages.title: On what date did %%[Defendant current name TE]%% sign the Affirmation of Defendant?
pages.text: On what date did %%[Defendant current name TE]%% sign the Affirmation of Defendant?

pages.title: The papers were filed with the court on %%DATE([Summons filed DA])%% and that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% signed the
pages.text:
The papers were filed with the court on %%DATE([Summons filed DA])%% and that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% signed the Affirmation of Defendant on %%DATE([Affidavit signed DA])%%.
Before %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can sign the Affirmation of Defendant, the papers must be filed with the court first. This means that the date %%[Defendant current name TE]%% signs the Affirmation should always be after the date of filing.

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% will have to sign the Affirmation of Defendant again.
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%% will have to sign the Affirmation of Defendant again.
After the Affirmation of Defendant has been signed and returned to you, you can come back and continue onto Part B: Getting Your Case on the Court Calendar.

pages.title: Were the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%, Notice of Automatic Orders, and Notice Concerning Continuation of Health Care Cover
pages.text:
Were the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%, Notice of Automatic Orders, and Notice Concerning Continuation of Health Care Coverage "served" on %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
This means that somebody (not you) personally delivered the papers to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.

pages.title: On what date were the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%, Notice of Automatic Orders, and Notice Concerning Continuation of Heal
pages.text: On what date were the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%, Notice of Automatic Orders, and Notice Concerning Continuation of Health Care Coverage "served" on %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: The papers were filed with the court on %%DATE([Summons filed DA])%% and that you served (delivered) the papers on %%DAT
pages.text:
The papers were filed with the court on %%DATE([Summons filed DA])%% and that you served (delivered) the papers on %%DATE([Summons served DA])%%.
Before you can serve the papers, they must be filed with the court first. This means that the date of service should always be after the date of filing.

pages.title: You will have to serve the papers on %%[Defendant current name TE]%% again.
pages.text:
You will have to serve the papers on %%[Defendant current name TE]%% again.
You have until %%DATE([Summons filed DA]+120)%% to serve the papers.
After the papers have been served on %%[Defendant current name TE]%% return to this form to continue onto Part B: Getting Your Case on the Court Calendar.

pages.title: Before you can continue, these papers must be personally delivered to ("served on") %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.
pages.text:
Before you can continue, these papers must be personally delivered to ("served on") %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.
You have until %%DATE([Summons served by DA])%%, 120 days from the date you filed the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%, to serve the papers on %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.
After the papers have been served on %%[Defendant current name TE]%% return to this form to continue onto Part B: Getting Your Case on the Court Calendar.

pages.title: There is a time limit for the service of the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% on a Defendant. The %%[Initiating papers TE]%% m
pages.text:
There is a time limit for the service of the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% on a Defendant. The %%[Initiating papers TE]%% must be served within 120 days from the date the divorce case was filed with the court.
Your case was filed with the court on %%DATE([Summons filed DA])%%. This means that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% should have been served with the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% before %%DATE([Summons filed DA]+120)%%.

pages.title: Did you get permission from the Court to have more time to serve the papers on %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
pages.text: Did you get permission from the Court to have more time to serve the papers on %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: On what date did the judge sign the Order to Extend Time to Serve?
pages.text: On what date did the judge sign the Order to Extend Time to Serve?

pages.title: You should include a copy of the Order that the Judge signed with your papers.
pages.text: You should include a copy of the Order that the Judge signed with your papers.

pages.title: Because you did not have the papers personally delivered to %%[Defendant current name TE]%% in time, you will have to ge
pages.text:
Because you did not have the papers personally delivered to %%[Defendant current name TE]%% in time, you will have to get permission from the Court to extend the time for service or buy another index number and file for divorce again.
Contact Brodsky Law or the court for more information.

pages.title: Were the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%, the Notice of Automatic Orders, and the Notice Concerning Continuation of Health Ca
pages.text: Were the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%, the Notice of Automatic Orders, and the Notice Concerning Continuation of Health Care Coverage personally delivered to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: The %%[Initiating papers TE]%% in a divorce case must be personally delivered to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.
pages.text:
The %%[Initiating papers TE]%% in a divorce case must be personally delivered to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%.
You have until %%DATE([Summons served by DA])%% to serve %%[Defendant current name TE]%% with the divorce papers from Part A: Starting Your Divorce Case. Remember that someone, not you, must serve the papers.
Click Exit and return after %%[Defendant current name TE]%% has been served.

pages.title: Who personally delivered the papers to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
pages.text: Who personally delivered the papers to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: The %%[Initiating papers TE]%% must be served by someone, but not you.
pages.text:
The %%[Initiating papers TE]%% must be served by someone, but not you.
From the date that you filed in court, you have 120 days to have the papers personally delivered to %%[Defendant current name TE]%%. This means that you have until %%DATE([Summons filed DA]+120)%%.

pages.title: Did you give the person who personally delivered the papers a fair and accurate photo of %%[Defendant current name TE]%%
pages.text: Did you give the person who personally delivered the papers a fair and accurate photo of %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: Remember to attach the photo of %%[Defendant current name TE]%% to the Affirmation of Service.
pages.text: Remember to attach the photo of %%[Defendant current name TE]%% to the Affirmation of Service.

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: Where was %%[Defendant current name TE]%% when the papers were personally delivered to %%[him/her Defendant TE]%%?
pages.text: Where was %%[Defendant current name TE]%% when the papers were personally delivered to %%[him/her Defendant TE]%%?

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% has %%[20/30 days TE]%%, until %%DATE([Defendant answer by DA])%%, to respond to the pap
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%% has %%[20/30 days TE]%%, until %%DATE([Defendant answer by DA])%%, to respond to the papers that were personally delivered.
Have you received a notice of appearance from %%[Defendant current name TE]%% or %%HISHER([Defendant gender])%% lawyer or from the court?

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% has %%[20/30 days TE]%% to answer the summons from the date that the papers were persona
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%% has %%[20/30 days TE]%% to answer the summons from the date that the papers were personally delivered.
This means that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% has until %%DATE([Defendant answer by DA])%% to return the Affirmation of Defendant to you or file an answer. If %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% does nothing by %%DATE([Defendant answer by DA])%% then %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% has defaulted and you can go to the next step.

pages.title: At this point, you can either:
pages.text:
At this point, you can either:
1. Wait until %%DATE([Defendant answer by DA])%% for %%[Defendant current name TE]%% to return the Affirmation of Defendant to you and then return here OR
2. If you think that %%[Defendant current name TE]%% will do nothing and default, you can print out the papers for the next step now. But, if you print out the papers for the next step now and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% does return the Affirmation of Defendant to you, you will have to return here and print out the papers again.
Would you like to Exit and return after %%DATE([Defendant answer by DA])%% or Continue and print out the papers for the next step?

pages.title: %%[Defendant current name TE]%% had %%[20/30 days TE]%% to answer the Summons.
pages.text:
%%[Defendant current name TE]%% had %%[20/30 days TE]%% to answer the Summons.
Because %%HESHE([Defendant gender])%% did not answer the Summons by %%DATE([Defendant answer by DA])%%, %%[Defendant current name TE]%% has defaulted.
You are ready for Part B: Getting Your Case on the Court Calendar. %%[Step 2 file date TE]%%

pages.title: I'm sorry but this form is no longer for you.
pages.text:
I'm sorry but this form is no longer for you.
Because %%[Defendant current name TE]%% or %%HISHER([Defendant gender])%% lawyer filed a Notice of Appearance, it is possible that you will not be able to get an uncontested divorce. You may want to contact the Supreme Court Clerk for more information.

pages.title: Do you have an Affirmation of Service filled out by the person who served the papers on %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?
pages.text: Do you have an Affirmation of Service filled out by the person who served the papers on %%[Defendant current name TE]%%?

pages.title: You started your case with a Summons With Notice.
pages.text:
You started your case with a Summons With Notice.
Do you have a completed Verified Complaint?

pages.title: What is the index number for your case?
pages.text: What is the index number for your case?

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: In what county did you file for divorce?
pages.text:
In what county did you file for divorce?
This can be found on the top of the %%[Initiating papers TE]%%.

pages.title: What was the basis of venue?
pages.text:
What was the basis of venue?
This information can be found at the top of the %%[Initiating papers TE]%% under "The basis of venue is:"

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: Finished
pages.text:
Finished
You have finished Part B: Getting Your Divorce Case on the Court Calendar.

pages.title: Also, after the divorce judgment is signed, both you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% may or may not be eligible for
pages.text: Also, after the divorce judgment is signed, both you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% may or may not be eligible for coverage under each other's health insurance plans.

pages.title: Here are a few things to remember:
pages.text:
Here are a few things to remember:
While the divorce is pending, both you and %%[Defendant current name TE]%% are under a court order that says that you can't do certain things.

pages.title: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't sell, mortgage or give away any property without permission from the court
pages.text: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't sell, mortgage or give away any property without permission from the court or the other person.

pages.title: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't transfer, give away or withdraw money from any retirement accounts or inves
pages.text: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't transfer, give away or withdraw money from any retirement accounts or investment accounts without permission from the court or the other person.

pages.title: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't go into unreasonable debt by borrowing money or using credit cards or cash
pages.text: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't go into unreasonable debt by borrowing money or using credit cards or cash advances.

pages.title: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't take each other off any existing medical, hospital and dental insurance cov
pages.text: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't take each other off any existing medical, hospital and dental insurance coverage and must keep the coverage you have current;

pages.title: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't change the beneficiaries of any existing life insurance policies and must k
pages.text: You or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% can't change the beneficiaries of any existing life insurance policies and must keep existing life insurance, automobile insurance, homeowners and renters insurance current.

pages.title: If you or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% get a notice of a tax lien, foreclosure, bankruptcy, or lawsuit or notice that
pages.text: If you or %%[Defendant current name TE]%% get a notice of a tax lien, foreclosure, bankruptcy, or lawsuit or notice that one has been filed, or that a bankruptcy stay has ended, the other person must be sent written notice about it within 10 days.

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: If you are at a public computer, remember to close this form and your document before leaving to protect your privacy. I
pages.text: If you are at a public computer, remember to close this form and your document before leaving to protect your privacy. If you don't know how, ask someone to help you. It is very important.

pages.title: IMPORTANT
pages.text:
IMPORTANT
Print on only one-side of the paper.

pages.title: Do you want a copy of the instructions printed in Spanish?
pages.text: Do you want a copy of the instructions printed in Spanish?

pages.title: When you finish, your Part B papers will be ready for download. Follow the on-screen instructions.
pages.text: When you finish, your Part B papers will be ready for download. Follow the on-screen instructions.

pages.title: Your name
pages.text:
Your name

Please enter your first and last name. This helps us identify your questionnaire when you submit.

pages.title: Children
pages.text:
Children

Do you have children from this marriage?

If yes, add each child's name, Social Security number, date of birth, and home address. Use + Add another child for additional children.

pages.title: Children's health coverage
pages.text:
Children's health coverage

If you have minor children from this marriage, answer whether they are covered by health insurance.

pages.title: Health coverage (you and spouse)
pages.text:
Health coverage (you and spouse)

If you or your spouse has coverage through an employer or union group health plan, provide the plan details below. If not applicable, enter N/A.

pages.title: Children — residence, custody, and support
pages.text:
Children — residence, custody, and support

Answer about children from this marriage. If any answer is Yes, complete the matching follow-up.

pages.title: Employment and military
pages.text:
Employment and military

Answer for yourself and your spouse. Estimates for annual income are fine.

pages.title: Birth, education, and prior marriages
pages.text:
Birth, education, and prior marriages

These details are often required on court forms. Enter your best answers; estimates are fine for dates.

pages.title: Please continue.
pages.text: Please continue.

pages.title: This form is not for you. A sheet will be printed with more information.
pages.text:
